input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** â€” use `AppThemeBinding` to provide values for `Light`, `Dark`, and optionally `Default`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the device theme at startup and listens for changes â€” no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** â€” use `SetAppTheme` or construct `AppThemeBinding` in code-behind:

```csharp
// Option 1: SetAppTheme extension method (simplest)
myLabel.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // light
    Colors.White);   // dark

// Option 2: Manual AppThemeBinding object
myPage.SetBinding(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    new AppThemeBinding
    {
        Light = Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
        Dark  = Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    });
```

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic** | Reacts to OS theme changes in real time â€” no manual subscription |
| **Fallback** | Set `Default` for when the OS theme is unresolved |
| **Resources** | Works with `StaticResource`/`DynamicResource` too â€” define themed colors in `App.xaml` resource dictionaries for reuse |
| **Force a theme** | `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` overrides the system setting |

For larger apps, define colors in `App.xaml` and reference them:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg">
    <!-- won't work directly; use merged dictionaries or: -->
</Color>

<!-- Better: use AppThemeBinding inline with resource colors -->
<Style TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps theme values centralized and your pages clean.
